---
title: Proteomic Data Overview Contaminants
subtitle: SpringBloom2021 - Genome-resolved Metaproteomic Dataset
date: 09.04.2025
author: Cyrill Hofer
output:
  rmdformats::readthedown:
    highlight: tango
    self_contained: true
    code_folding: hide
    thumbnails: true
    gallery: false
    fig_width: 15
    df_print: kable
    fig_caption: yes
    use_bookdown: true
    css: custom.css
---

# Data Preparation
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, paged.print=FALSE}
# Clear R's Brain
rm(list = ls())

# Set the working directory
setwd("/SynologyDrive/PhD/Projects/SpringBloom2021/DataAnalysis_SpringBloom2021/Figure_02/ReplicateRecovery/")

# Load the libraries
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(DT)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)
library(scales)
library(forcats)

# Load the color palette
source("ColorPalette.R")

# Load the data
dat <- read_csv(file = "Proteomic_Data_Overview_Level_Long.csv")
species_dat <- read_csv("../../../Data/Species_Data/Final_Data/Species_Table.csv")
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Script: Proportion & Abundance Distributions for Protein-Level Data
#          (Linear abundance scale)
# =============================================================================

# 1) Load libraries (assumes `dat` is already in your environment)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)  # to combine plots
library(scales)     # for comma_format()

# 2) Filter to proteins and recode presence
protein_df <- dat %>%
  filter(Level == "Protein",
         Abundance > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    Replicate = factor(
      Presence,
      levels = c("one", "both"),
      labels = c("One Replicate", "Both Replicates")
    )
  )

# 3) Compute percent of proteins in each category
prop_df <- protein_df %>%
  count(Replicate) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100)

# 4) Bar plot of proportions
p_prop <- ggplot(prop_df, aes(x = Replicate, y = pct, fill = Replicate)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", width = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "One Replicate"   = ColorPalette[4],
    "Both Replicates" = ColorPalette[2]
  )) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(round(pct,1), "%")),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 4) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent of Proteins",
    title = "Proteins Detected in One vs. Both Replicates"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# 5) Violin + jitter of abundance by replicate category (linear scale)
p_dist <- ggplot(protein_df, aes(x = Replicate, y = Abundance, fill = Replicate)) +
  geom_violin(trim = TRUE, alpha = 0.4, color = "black", width = 0.8) +
  geom_jitter(
    shape  = 21,
    aes(fill = Replicate),
    color  = "black",
    size   = 1.5,
    width  = 0.2,
    height = 0,
    alpha  = 0.7
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "One Replicate"   = ColorPalette[4],
    "Both Replicates" = ColorPalette[2]
  )) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = comma_format(),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "IBAQ Abundance",
    title = "Abundance of Proteins by Detection Replicate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.y    = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title      = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold")
  )

# 6) Combine vertically (proportion above, abundance below)
combined_plot <- p_prop / p_dist + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2))

# 7) Display
print(combined_plot)

# 8) Save to A4‐portrait PDF
ggsave(
  filename = "Protein_Replicate_Overview_Linear.pdf",
  plot     = combined_plot,
  width    = 8.27,
  height   = 11.69,
  units    = "in",
  device   = "pdf"
)
# =============================================================================

```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Script: Sample‐level % Proteins vs. % Signal in One‐ vs. Both‐Replicate
# =============================================================================

# 1) Load libraries (assumes `dat` is the “long” CSV you loaded at top)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)    # for percent_format()
library(forcats)

# 2) Summarize at the protein level by sample:
#    - count of distinct proteins in “one” vs “both”
#    - summed abundance in “one” vs “both”
prot_summ <- dat %>%
  filter(Level == "Protein",
         Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Date, Presence) %>%
  summarise(
    count     = n_distinct(ID),
    abundance = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # 3) compute percentages within each sample
  group_by(Date) %>%
  mutate(
    percent_count     = 100 * count / sum(count),
    percent_abundance = 100 * abundance / sum(abundance)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# 4) Pivot into “Metric”-long form for plotting
plot_df <- prot_summ %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(percent_count, percent_abundance),
    names_to  = "Metric",
    values_to = "Percent"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(
      Metric,
      levels = c("percent_count", "percent_abundance"),
      labels = c("% Proteins", "% Abundance")
    ),
    Presence = factor(
      Presence,
      levels = c("one", "both"),
      labels = c("One Replicate", "Both Replicates")
    ),
    Date = factor(Date, levels = unique(Date))  # preserve original order
  )

# 5) Plot: stacked bars of % Proteins vs. % Abundance, one facet per Metric
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Date, y = Percent, fill = Presence)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", width = 0.8, position = "stack") +
  facet_wrap(~ Metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("One Replicate" = ColorPalette[4],
               "Both Replicates" = ColorPalette[2])
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(scale = 1)) +
  labs(
    x    = "Sample Date",
    y    = "Percent",
    fill = "Detected in:"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    strip.text       = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.y      = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title       = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title     = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text      = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.spacing.y  = unit(1, "lines")
  )

# 6) (Optional) Save as PDF
ggsave(
  "Replicate_Percentage_Comparison.pdf",
  width  = 12,
  height = 8,
  units  = "in"
)
# =============================================================================

```

```{r}
# ================== Per-sample & pooled replicate summaries (with abundance overlap) ==================
library(dplyr)

# Build a clean protein table (labels normalized), keep >0 abundances
protein_df <- dat %>%
  filter(Level == "Protein") %>%
  mutate(
    Abundance = as.numeric(Abundance),
    Presence  = case_when(
      tolower(Presence) %in% c("both","both replicates","both_replicates") ~ "both",
      tolower(Presence) %in% c("one","one replicate","one_replicate")      ~ "one",
      TRUE ~ tolower(Presence)
    ),
    Replicate = factor(Presence, levels = c("one","both"),
                       labels = c("One Replicate","Both Replicates"))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Abundance), Abundance > 0)

# ---- A) Per-sample abundance shares (ONE & BOTH) ----
per_sample_abund <- protein_df %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  summarise(
    total_abund     = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
    one_abund       = sum(Abundance[Replicate == "One Replicate"],  na.rm = TRUE),
    both_abund      = sum(Abundance[Replicate == "Both Replicates"], na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_one_abund   = 100 * one_abund  / total_abund,
    pct_both_abund  = 100 * both_abund / total_abund,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Medians / ranges for abundance overlap
median_one_abund  <- median(per_sample_abund$pct_one_abund,  na.rm = TRUE)
range_one_abund   <- range(per_sample_abund$pct_one_abund,   na.rm = TRUE)
mean_one_abund    <- mean(per_sample_abund$pct_one_abund,    na.rm = TRUE)

median_both_abund <- median(per_sample_abund$pct_both_abund, na.rm = TRUE)
range_both_abund  <- range(per_sample_abund$pct_both_abund,  na.rm = TRUE)
mean_both_abund   <- mean(per_sample_abund$pct_both_abund,   na.rm = TRUE)

# ---- B) Per-sample % of IDs in ONE/BOTH (counts) ----
per_sample_counts <- protein_df %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  summarise(
    total_ids     = n_distinct(ID),
    both_ids      = n_distinct(ID[Replicate == "Both Replicates"]),
    one_ids       = total_ids - both_ids,
    pct_both_ids  = 100 * both_ids / total_ids,  # "overlap for counts"
    pct_one_ids   = 100 * one_ids  / total_ids,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

median_overlap_ct <- median(per_sample_counts$pct_both_ids, na.rm = TRUE)
range_overlap_ct  <- range(per_sample_counts$pct_both_ids,  na.rm = TRUE)
median_one_ids    <- median(per_sample_counts$pct_one_ids,  na.rm = TRUE)
range_one_ids     <- range(per_sample_counts$pct_one_ids,   na.rm = TRUE)

# ---- C) Per-sample median fold (Both / One) ----
per_sample_fold <- protein_df %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  summarise(
    med_both = if (any(Replicate == "Both Replicates"))
      median(Abundance[Replicate == "Both Replicates"], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    med_one  = if (any(Replicate == "One Replicate"))
      median(Abundance[Replicate == "One Replicate"], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
    fold_both_over_one = ifelse(is.finite(med_both) & is.finite(med_one) & med_one > 0,
                                med_both / med_one, NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

valid_folds     <- per_sample_fold$fold_both_over_one[is.finite(per_sample_fold$fold_both_over_one)]
n_valid_folds   <- length(valid_folds)
n_total_samples <- nrow(per_sample_fold)
median_fold_ps  <- median(valid_folds, na.rm = TRUE)
range_fold_ps   <- range(valid_folds,  na.rm = TRUE)
mean_fold_ps    <- mean(valid_folds,   na.rm = TRUE)

# ---- D) Pooled medians & shares (dataset-wide) ----
pooled_median_both <- protein_df %>%
  filter(Replicate == "Both Replicates") %>%
  summarise(m = median(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(m)

pooled_median_one <- protein_df %>%
  filter(Replicate == "One Replicate") %>%
  summarise(m = median(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% pull(m)

pooled_fold   <- pooled_median_both / pooled_median_one
pooled_lower  <- pooled_median_one  / pooled_median_both

total_abund_all <- sum(protein_df$Abundance, na.rm = TRUE)
one_abund_all   <- sum(protein_df$Abundance[protein_df$Replicate == "One Replicate"],  na.rm = TRUE)
both_abund_all  <- sum(protein_df$Abundance[protein_df$Replicate == "Both Replicates"], na.rm = TRUE)
pooled_pct_one_abund  <- 100 * one_abund_all  / total_abund_all
pooled_pct_both_abund <- 100 * both_abund_all / total_abund_all  # "pooled abundance overlap"

# ---- E) Optional dataset-wide ID context ----
by_id <- protein_df %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  summarise(
    any_one  = any(Replicate == "One Replicate"),
    any_both = any(Replicate == "Both Replicates"),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
pct_ids_any_one       <- 100 * mean(by_id$any_one)
pct_ids_only_ever_one <- 100 * mean(by_id$any_one & !by_id$any_both)
pct_rows_one          <- 100 * mean(protein_df$Replicate == "One Replicate")

# ---- F) Print nicely ----
cat(sprintf("Counts overlap (BOTH IDs) per sample: median = %.1f%% (range %.1f–%.1f%%)\n",
            median_overlap_ct, range_overlap_ct[1], range_overlap_ct[2]))
cat(sprintf("ONE-replicate IDs per sample: median = %.1f%% (range %.1f–%.1f%%)\n",
            median_one_ids, range_one_ids[1], range_one_ids[2]))

cat(sprintf("Abundance overlap (BOTH) per sample: median = %.1f%% (range %.1f–%.1f%%); mean = %.1f%%\n",
            median_both_abund, range_both_abund[1], range_both_abund[2], mean_both_abund))
cat(sprintf("ONE-replicate abundance per sample: median = %.1f%% (range %.1f–%.1f%%); mean = %.1f%%\n",
            median_one_abund,  range_one_abund[1],  range_one_abund[2],  mean_one_abund))

cat(sprintf("Per-sample median fold (Both/One): median = %.1f× (range %.1f–%.1f×); n = %d/%d samples\n",
            median_fold_ps, range_fold_ps[1], range_fold_ps[2], n_valid_folds, n_total_samples))
cat(sprintf("Pooled medians (Both/One): %.1f×; One is %.1fx of Both\n",
            pooled_fold, pooled_lower))

cat(sprintf("Pooled abundance overlap (BOTH): %.1f%%; pooled ONE-replicate abundance: %.1f%%\n",
            pooled_pct_both_abund, pooled_pct_one_abund))

# Optional extra context:
cat(sprintf("Rows detected in ONE replicate (row-level): %.1f%%\n", pct_rows_one))
cat(sprintf("Unique protein IDs with ≥1 ONE-replicate detection (ANY-ONE): %.1f%%; ONLY-EVER ONE: %.1f%%\n",
            pct_ids_any_one, pct_ids_only_ever_one))
# ======================================================================================================

```


# check if proteins with less replicate overlap are also less even with a few very dominating proteins
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(vegan)   

species_eveness_dat <- species_dat %>%
  select(Date, Species_ID, Species_Abundance) %>%
  distinct()

# Build abundance matrix
sp_mat_df <- species_eveness_dat %>%
  mutate(Species_Abundance = as.numeric(Species_Abundance)) %>%
  group_by(Date, Species_ID) %>%
  summarise(Species_Abundance = median(Species_Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = Species_ID,
    values_from = Species_Abundance,
    values_fill = 0
  )

date_vec <- sp_mat_df$Date
sp_mat <- sp_mat_df %>%
  select(-Date) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Replace any NA with 0
sp_mat[is.na(sp_mat)] <- 0

# Calculate richness, Shannon, and Pielou's evenness
richness <- specnumber(sp_mat)
shannon  <- diversity(sp_mat, index = "shannon")
pielou   <- ifelse(richness > 0, shannon / log(richness), NA_real_)

species_eveness_out <- data.frame(
  Date     = date_vec,
  Richness = richness,
  Shannon  = shannon,
  Pielou   = pielou
)

# --- Total proteins detected per sample ---
prot_counts <- dat %>%
  filter(Level == "Protein", Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Proteins = n_distinct(ID),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- % proteins found in both replicates (coverage) ---
overlap_df2 <- dat %>%
  filter(Level == "Protein", Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  summarise(
    total_ids   = n_distinct(ID),
    both_ids    = n_distinct(ID[Presence == "both"]),
    Percent_Covered = both_ids / total_ids * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# --- Merge with species evenness table ---
species_eveness_out <- species_eveness_out %>%
  left_join(prot_counts,   by = "Date") %>%
  left_join(overlap_df2,   by = "Date")

species_eveness_out

# =============================================================================
# Correlation tests on combined table
# =============================================================================
library(corrr)   # nice correlation matrices (optional)

# Keep only numeric columns for correlation
cor_dat <- species_eveness_out %>%
  select(Richness, Shannon, Pielou, Total_Proteins, Percent_Covered)

# Pairwise Spearman correlations
cor_mat <- cor(cor_dat, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Print correlation matrix
print(round(cor_mat, 3))

# Individual correlation tests (with p-values)
ct_rich_prot   <- cor.test(species_eveness_out$Total_Proteins,
                           species_eveness_out$Richness,
                           method = "spearman")
ct_even_prot   <- cor.test(species_eveness_out$Total_Proteins,
                           species_eveness_out$Pielou,
                           method = "spearman")
ct_cover_prot  <- cor.test(species_eveness_out$Total_Proteins,
                           species_eveness_out$Percent_Covered,
                           method = "spearman")
ct_cover_rich  <- cor.test(species_eveness_out$Percent_Covered,
                           species_eveness_out$Richness,
                           method = "spearman")
ct_cover_even  <- cor.test(species_eveness_out$Percent_Covered,
                           species_eveness_out$Pielou,
                           method = "spearman")

# Print results nicely
cat("Total proteins vs richness: rho =",
    round(ct_rich_prot$estimate, 3), " p =", signif(ct_rich_prot$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("Total proteins vs evenness: rho =",
    round(ct_even_prot$estimate, 3), " p =", signif(ct_even_prot$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("Total proteins vs % covered: rho =",
    round(ct_cover_prot$estimate, 3), " p =", signif(ct_cover_prot$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("% covered vs richness: rho =",
    round(ct_cover_rich$estimate, 3), " p =", signif(ct_cover_rich$p.value, 3), "\n")
cat("% covered vs evenness: rho =",
    round(ct_cover_even$estimate, 3), " p =", signif(ct_cover_even$p.value, 3), "\n")
```

# publication ready figure

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# FINAL: Plot + Summary tables
# - Plot: % Proteins (LEFT), then % Abundance (RIGHT)
# - Blue bottom, Yellow top (reverse stacking)
# - Error bar at blue–yellow interface = mean ± SD of boundary across samples
# - Hard-coded: ONLY Presence in c("both","one") (ignore "none")
#
# Summary outputs:
# 1) Percent summaries (per-sample %) by Metric x Presence:
#    mean, median, sd, IQR, min, max
# 2) Absolute summaries (per-sample) by Metric x Presence:
#    counts: #proteins per date (n_distinct ID)
#    abundance: summed abundance per date
#    mean, median, sd, IQR, min, max
# 3) Per-date table (wide): counts + abundance + percentages for both/one
# =============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ---- 1) Hard filter: ONLY BOTH vs ONE ----
protein_df <- dat %>%
  filter(
    Level == "Protein",
    Abundance > 0,
    Presence %in% c("both", "one")
  )

# ---- 2) Per-date (sample) absolute summaries by Presence ----
# This is what you asked for: how many proteins and summed abundance per date.
per_date_abs <- protein_df %>%
  group_by(Date, Presence) %>%
  summarise(
    protein_count = n_distinct(ID),
    abundance_sum = sum(Abundance),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- 3) Per-date percentages derived from the absolute numbers ----
per_date <- per_date_abs %>%
  group_by(Date) %>%
  mutate(
    percent_count     = 100 * protein_count / sum(protein_count),
    percent_abundance = 100 * abundance_sum / sum(abundance_sum)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# ---- 4) Long format for plotting (% only) ----
plot_df <- per_date %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(percent_count, percent_abundance),
    names_to  = "Metric",
    values_to = "Percent"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Plot order requested: % Proteins first, then % Abundance
    Metric = factor(
      Metric,
      levels = c("percent_count", "percent_abundance"),
      labels = c("% Proteins", "% Abundance")
    ),
    Presence = factor(
      Presence,
      levels = c("both", "one"),
      labels = c("Both Replicates", "One Replicate")
    )
  )

# ---- 5) Mean stacked bar segments (mean % across dates) ----
bar_means <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(Metric, Presence) %>%
  summarise(mean_pct = mean(Percent, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# ---- 6) Boundary SD at the interface (TOP of BLUE) ----
# With reverse stacking, interface height = 100 - (% One Replicate)
one_sd <- plot_df %>%
  filter(Presence == "One Replicate") %>%
  group_by(Metric) %>%
  summarise(
    one_mean = mean(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    one_sd   = sd(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

boundary_sd <- one_sd %>%
  transmute(
    Metric,
    boundary_mean = 100 - one_mean,
    ymin = pmax(0, boundary_mean - one_sd),
    ymax = pmin(100, boundary_mean + one_sd)
  )

# ---- 7) Plot ----
p <- ggplot(bar_means, aes(x = Metric, y = mean_pct, fill = Presence)) +
  # stacked mean bars
  geom_col(
    width     = 0.62,
    color     = "black",
    linewidth = 0.35,
    position  = position_stack(reverse = TRUE)  # blue bottom, yellow top
  ) +
  # boundary error bars
  geom_errorbar(
    data        = boundary_sd,
    aes(x = Metric, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    width       = 0.10,
    linewidth   = 0.9,
    color       = "black"
  ) +
  # publication-consistent colors (match your style: fixed hex, no legend)
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Both Replicates" = "#7aa5c3",  # <- set your preferred blue here
      "One Replicate"   = "#f2b705"   # <- set your preferred yellow here
      # If you want to reuse your existing palette instead, swap to ColorPalette[2]/[4]
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  # Y axis like your other plot (explicit limits + breaks)
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 25),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Metric",
    y = "Percent"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title      = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x    = element_blank()
  )

print(p)

ggsave("ReplicateOverlap_TwoBars_BoundarySD_FINAL.pdf",
       p, width = 4, height = 4.5, units = "in")

# =============================================================================
# SUMMARY TABLES
# =============================================================================

# Helper: summary stats for a numeric vector
summ_stats <- function(x) {
  c(
    mean   = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr    = IQR(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    min    = min(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
}

# ---- A) Percent summary stats (across dates), by Metric x Presence ----
percent_summary <- plot_df %>%
  group_by(Metric, Presence) %>%
  summarise(
    mean   = mean(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd     = sd(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr    = IQR(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    min    = min(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    max    = max(Percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(Metric, Presence)

# ---- B) Absolute (count + abundance) summary stats (across dates), by Presence ----
# Counts = proteins per date; Abundance = summed abundance per date
abs_summary <- per_date %>%
  mutate(
    Presence = factor(Presence,
                      levels = c("both", "one"),
                      labels = c("Both Replicates", "One Replicate"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(Presence) %>%
  summarise(
    # protein counts per date
    proteins_mean   = mean(protein_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    proteins_median = median(protein_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    proteins_sd     = sd(protein_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    proteins_iqr    = IQR(protein_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    proteins_min    = min(protein_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    proteins_max    = max(protein_count, na.rm = TRUE),

    # abundance sums per date
    abund_mean   = mean(abundance_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_median = median(abundance_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_sd     = sd(abundance_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_iqr    = IQR(abundance_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_min    = min(abundance_sum, na.rm = TRUE),
    abund_max    = max(abundance_sum, na.rm = TRUE),

    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---- C) Per-date table (counts + abundance + percentages), wide format ----
per_date_wide <- per_date %>%
  mutate(
    Presence = ifelse(Presence == "both", "Both", "One")
  ) %>%
  select(Date, Presence, protein_count, abundance_sum, percent_count, percent_abundance) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Presence,
    values_from = c(protein_count, abundance_sum, percent_count, percent_abundance)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Date)

# Print tables
percent_summary
abs_summary
per_date_wide

# Optional saves:
write.csv(percent_summary, "ReplicateOverlap_PercentSummary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(abs_summary,     "ReplicateOverlap_AbsoluteSummary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(per_date_wide,   "ReplicateOverlap_PerDateTable.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```
